title: thesomewhatyou's Horror Hell
description: You've entered what seems like hell. Welcome to your fate.
body:
Welcome to hell. Paradise for madmen.

# Don't want something cryptic, don't we? 

Fine.

Welcome to thesomewhatyou's Horror Hell. 10 mods including libraries. You can never relax again. Your fate awaits, my friend.

# What's the purpose of this? Why is it so... different from what you usually make?

Well, why not? Everyone needs a scare. Entering this modpack, you'll meet scaries and creepypastas alike. In this modpack, you fight to survive, or hide. It all depends on you. 

To be absolutely real, I tripped a lot on this. College, TOSCR-project, all that good stuff. So here's your treat. Horror. It's not really a "treat" per se, but it's more or so trick or trick. And yes, a spoooooky scary Halloween version will be released once it's Halloween and I decide "Huh. I wanna make a bad push today!" and it's most likely gonna be full release on Halloween. 

This modpack includes shaders which can be **extremely intensive on your machine,** and unlike TOSCR-project, this modpack isn't as sparing on your machine as in resources. I highly suggest of you to NOT play this on a garbage computer. If your computer can't run shaders on medium settings consider a new modpack.

This uses Forge 1.19.2 which is fairly straightforward and all that. Like TOSCR, plop it in ~~PolyMC~~ Prism Launcher or their respective forks and done. You now have hell sitting on your Instances. I suggest you promptly run.

This essentially uses the same license TOSCR-project uses. This also doesn't come with a warranty so if this thing eats your homework (or your PC) not my problem. 


# Does this mean TOSCR will get less updates?

Yes and no. TOSCR is still having active development (For me, looking at the moon in TOSCR gives you 1300 FPS. I think that's proof) but THH is... debatable. Like I said, big release on Halloween. I'm planning to make it, but most horror mods usually all have ARR. These mods are the best mods that I oculd find, and the greatest that exist that don't have a restrictive license.
I am gaining some mods slowly, but October is 2 months away. I'm staying down for a bit due to some problems in life, but the next TOSCR release is gonna slow down.

Credits to all the modmen:
The Anomaly by Clorofite, MCreator, Sounds by Mazellmi
Antlers by HP, MCreator
Box of Horrors by Kuro Modding
Catalogue by MrCrayfish
cave_dweller by Cadentem
Creeping Woods by User, MCreator
The One Who Watches by Pixel Coder

The rest of the mods are just libraries and some needed things.
